mechanism_narrative: Sustained increases in nonfarm payrolls signal broad economic strength and rising corporate revenues, leading to positive revisions in earnings expectations and driving equity prices higher over the short term.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS has shown consistent positive growth through 2017
failure_condition: A positive 3-month change in PAYEMS is followed by negative S&P 500 returns more than 50% of the time over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the effective federal funds rate rises above its 12-month moving average, it confirms a monetary tightening cycle is underway. This increases the discount rate for future earnings and tightens financial conditions, leading to negative equity returns.
evidence_refs: fact:The Fed has raised rates multiple times in 2017
failure_condition: The signal's information coefficient (IC) is not statistically significant (p > 0.05) over the full backtest period.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in retail sales (RSAFS) and a rise in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) indicates a severe contraction in consumer health, a leading indicator of recession. This joint signal precedes sharp declines in equity markets.
candidate_trigger: 3-month change in RSAFS < 0 AND 3-month change in UNRATE > 0.2
failure_condition: In periods where the trigger condition is met, the S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 40% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion with low unemployment, a positive surprise in industrial production growth signals potential economic overheating. This increases the probability of aggressive Fed tightening, causing a negative reaction in equity markets.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE is at a cyclical low of 4.1%
failure_condition: The correlation between the signal and forward returns is positive during periods identified as late-cycle expansions.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating housing starts (HOUST) reflect strong consumer balance sheets and confidence, acting as a leading indicator for broader economic activity and construction-related corporate earnings, thus positively impacting equity prices.
failure_condition: The rank correlation between the 6-month change in HOUST and 60-day forward S&P 500 returns is less than 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An acceleration in consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) while real GDP growth (GDPC1) is decelerating creates a stagflationary environment. This squeezes corporate margins and limits the central bank's ability to provide monetary support, leading to negative equity returns.
candidate_trigger: Year-over-year % change in CPIAUCSL > 2.5% AND quarter-over-quarter % change in GDPC1 is declining
failure_condition: S&P 500 returns are not statistically significantly lower during triggered periods compared to non-triggered periods (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below a key psychological and potentially structural level (e.g., 4.2%), it signals maximum labor market tightness. This fuels wage inflation fears and expectations of imminent Fed hawkishness, causing a short-term negative impact on equities.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE is 4.1% as of the latest reading, having recently crossed this threshold
failure_condition: Following a trigger, the average 5-day S&P 500 return is non-negative over a 10-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Positive quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP (GDPC1) is the most direct signal of an expanding economy. This translates directly into higher aggregate corporate earnings, supporting higher equity valuations over a medium-term horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Latest GDPC1 reading shows continued economic expansion
failure_condition: The long-run correlation between quarterly real GDP growth and subsequent 60-day S&P 500 returns is statistically indistinguishable from zero.
